headline: Oil Royalty Dispute Reaches RM300 Billion Mark
context: Sarawak estimates cumulative losses of RM300 billion from the 5% oil royalty cap versus the 20% demanded under MA63 terms.

cards.big: Sarawak claims RM300 billion in lost oil royalties since 1974
cards.sub: The 5% royalty rate has been a flashpoint between Petronas and Borneo states

cards.big: Petronas paid RM4.8 billion to Sarawak in 2025 royalties alone
cards.sub: At 20% the payment would have been approximately RM19.2 billion last year

cards.big: Sabah received only RM1.2 billion in 2025 petroleum royalties
cards.sub: State GDP per capita remains 30% below Peninsula average despite oil wealth

cards.big: Federal government offered SST on petroleum products as compromise in 2023
cards.sub: Sarawak collected RM5.2 billion from state sales tax but wants both mechanisms

cards.big: The royalty gap exposes whether resource extraction serves local or central interests
At 5% versus 20%, the RM300 billion gap measures federalism's broken promise to Borneo.
